title: Lithocarpus mabesae
taxonomy: Lithocarpus mabesae is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the phylum Tracheophyta and class Magnoliopsida. It belongs to the family Fagaceae, which consists of various species of beeches and oak trees. The genus Lithocarpus is a group of deciduous and evergreen trees native to Southeast Asia, commonly known as the stone oaks. Lithocarpus mabesae is a species of stone oak that is native to the Philippines. It is further classified within the order Fagales, which includes other species of trees with similar characteristics.
uses: Lithocarpus mabesae has been used in traditional medicine in the Philippines, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves and bark are used to make tea that is said to alleviate fever and other symptoms of illness. In addition, it is also used as a treatment for digestive issues and to promote wound healing. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Further studies are needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Lithocarpus mabesae.

intro: Lithocarpus mabesae, commonly known as the Philippine tan, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family (Fagaceae). The health benefits of Lithocarpus mabesae include its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce inflammation, which may help to prevent cardiovascular disease and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lithocarpus mabesae is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues.
